name: Sweet Potato Pancakes
snippet: We love Sweet Potatoes!  We bake them, roast them, steam them, mash them, pan fry them, you name it!  They are a staple in our diets and a fixed item on our shopping list.  We enjoy them as a side dish, but they are also a smart choice for baking because of their natural sweetness.  I can tell you, they surely do not disappoint in this version of clean pancakes.  Try it for yourself!

name: Skewered Chicken
snippet: I am not a fan of cherry pies or other cherry flavored foods but I do enjoy fresh cherries!  I picked up some sweet Washington cherries from the store and I have been enjoying the fresh fruit so much that I was inspired to create something with them.  I knew I wanted something quick and simple so I decided to make a sauce.  The cherries,  jalapenos and soy sauce work well together to create perfect balance.  I usually avoid soy, but I feel it’s okay to have from time to time; besides soy sauce provides a unique flavor that you can’t really duplicate.  I thought it would be fun to make chicken skewers and use the sauce as a glaze, kind of reminiscent of satay.  I am so glad I did because I really enjoyed these!  Fresh, simple ingredients make the best dishes in my opinion and this is a testament to that.  Let me know if you try it, I would love to know what you think!

name: Mushroom and Kale Frittata
snippet: What comes to mind when you think of brunch?  For me, it is usually an omelet or frittata.  I love egg dishes because you can pretty much add any vegetables or protein you desire and they still turn out delicious.  This time I opted for a meatless frittata.  I had just picked up some kale from the farmer’s market and I knew I wanted to incorporate it so I decided to go with a vegetable version.  I love the combination of kale and mushroom and with the sweetness from the potatoes, the flavors were perfectly balanced!

name: Spiced Pumpkin Cookies
snippet: Fall is my favorite time of year. I love the crispness of the air, changing of the leaves, wearing fashionable boots and jackets, but most off all, I love everything PUMPKIN! Pumpkin pie, pumpkin donuts, pumpkin muffins, pumpkin lattes, pumpkin bread, pumpkin cake, pumpkin ice cream, pumpkin cheesecake and I can’t forget, pumpkin rolls. I feel a sugar coma coming on just typing this! Given my love for the gourd, I knew I had to create a clean pumpkin treat that I could enjoy this time of year and not feel guilt or shame. I am so glad that I did, but now I have to keep myself eating the entire batch! They are that good. If you are obsessed with pumpkin like I am, you will definitely want to make these. Without further ado…introducing, Spiced Pumpkin Cookies!!!

name: Guacamole
snippet: If you don’t already know this about me, I have a love affair with avocados.  I’ll take them any way I can get them!  Straight up with a little salt and pepper, sliced or diced on salads, on tacos, in dressings, and my all time favorite, Guacamole! In my opinion, Chipotle makes the best guacamole but mine is a close second ;) .  This is my basic, fail proof, easy, go to recipe that never disappoints.  It’s good as is but feel free to jazz it up with some of the variations listed below.  Give it a try and let me know what you think.

name: Hot Chocolate
snippet: As we enter into the colder months, it’s nice to know that I can still enjoy a hot cup of Cocoa. Making it with coconut milk and stevia means that I don’t feel guilty for indulging.  This is a very easy recipe that you can make for yourself, family or guests.  Try it and let me know what you think!

name: Braised Country Style Ribs
snippet: I love Chicken, Fish and Turkey, but admittedly I grow tired of the usual suspects from time to time.  I told Joe that I wanted to mix it up a bit so we picked up a pack of Country Style Pork Ribs from the grocery store.  These are not typical ribs with bones that you eat with your fingers.  They are essentially shoulder (Boston) butts cut into strips and require a knife and fork.  Nevertheless, they are tasty grilled but even better in my opinion, cooked low and slow in the oven.  That is exactly how I decided to serve them up.  These ribs have just the right amount of fat, allowing them to come out juicy and tender after 3 to 4 hours of braising.  A classic comfort dish for sure, and just perfect for the colder months.  I am already looking forward to leftovers!

name: Barbecue Sauce
snippet: I wanted to share with you a quick, tasty and Clean Barbecue Sauce that I developed.  Store bought barbecue sauces are full of added sugars, preservatives and chemicals.  I don’t know about you, but I feel much better knowing what I am putting in my body.  I created a clean version using fresh ingredients that takes literally 20 minutes to make.  Try it out the next time you fire up the grill!

name: Squash Fritters
snippet: I love Squash and Zucchini.  Usually I sautee the vegetables with onions in a little olive oil. However, today I felt like trying something a little different.  I thought it would be fun to use the flavors I enjoy but to mix it up a bit.  I picked up some Mexican squash from the grocery store and figured there was no time like the present to experiment.  I used a recipe similar to my corn fritters, but tweaked it just a little.  These were so good, nice and crispy on the outside and very soft, almost creamy in the middle.  The thyme made the fritters very fragrant and added another layer of flavor that I really enjoyed.  I definitely plan on making these again, I may even add them to my Thanksgiving menu this year.  If you are tired of the same ol’ sides, try these and let me know what you think!

name: Savory Crepes
snippet: I love Crêpes but I have always been too intimidated to make them.   I decided it was finally time to face my fears and try my hand at the French pancakes for Test Kitchen Tuesday.  I cannot believe how easy they were to make, I should have tried this a long time ago!  Usually I opt for sweet crêpes, but today I was in the mood for something savory and filling.  I think I may have found the perfect lunch or light dinner option for summer.  Substituting Brown Rice Flour and Coconut Milk gave me a clean version I could enjoy guilt-free and the addition of fresh herbs provided added flavor and aroma.  I can’t wait to make these again, I am already brainstorming all of the variations I can try!  I am providing the recipe for the filling I used but feel free to use any filling and or dressing you desire.

name: Vegetable Chili
snippet: As you can probably tell, I am all about hearty soups during the fall and this vegetable chili is no exception.  It is so hearty that you won’t even miss the meat!  That is why it fits in so well with our Meatless Monday meals (although I wouldn’t mind having this any day of the week). This is also one of those dishes that tastes better the longer the flavors mingle so leftovers will be extra delicious.  You can make it as spicy or mild as you like by adjusting the cayenne and including some or all of the jalapeno seeds.  Go ahead make a big batch, freeze it and pull it out on those days when you don’t feel like cooking, you’ll be glad you did!
